title: carex capillaris subsp. major
intro: Carex capillaris subsp. major, also known as the hairy sedge, is a subspecies of sedge that belongs to the Carex genus and is native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, the hairy sedge has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, due to its anthelmintic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is also believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and serving as a diuretic. In herbal preparations, the hairy sedge is often used in infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although its use should be guided by a qualified healthcare professional.
